name: Maria Santos
bio: Compassionate volunteer with over a decade of supporting elders through daily routines and mobility challenges. Calm, patient, and a wonderful listener.

reviews.author: Aisha R.
reviews.text: Maria treated my grandmother like her own. She would sing to her in the afternoons and remember every little preference.

reviews.author: Mohammed K.
reviews.text: Endlessly patient with my father after his fall. Never rushing, always kind.

reviews.author: Hala M.
reviews.text: I cannot recommend her enough. My aunt now smiles when she hears Maria at the door.

reviews.author: Faisal A.
reviews.text: Reliable beyond words. Learned my father's favourite Filipino dishes too.

notifications.message: has requested you for weekend companionship visits.
would like meal-preparation support twice a week.

name: Aarti Sharma
bio: Trained nurse turned home volunteer. Specialises in medication reminders and post-surgery recovery support. Speaks softly, listens deeply.

reviews.author: Rajesh V.
reviews.text: Aarti's nursing background was invaluable during my mother's recovery from hip surgery.

reviews.author: Yasmin H.
reviews.text: She speaks to my mother in Hindi — such a comfort. Their afternoons feel like old friends catching up.

reviews.author: Khaled M.
reviews.text: Trustworthy, calm in emergencies, and treats my father with such dignity.

reviews.author: Noor S.
reviews.text: She remembered my grandmother's birthday and brought her a small flower.

notifications.message: family asking about post-stroke medication management.
would like additional Hindi-speaking nurse for medication review.

name: Fatima Al-Hashimi
bio: Local volunteer who deeply understands cultural and dietary preferences. Warm, respectful, and skilled at companionship-led care.

reviews.author: Mariam K.
reviews.text: Fatima cooks the same dishes my mother grew up with. Their Arabic conversations bring my mother so much joy.

reviews.author: Hassan A.
reviews.text: She understands our family's customs without us having to explain. Respectful, modest, deeply kind.

reviews.author: Amna L.
reviews.text: Patient and gentle. My grandmother lights up when she walks in.

reviews.author: Abdullah R.
reviews.text: Fatima helped my wife regain her confidence after she stopped going out.

notifications.message: invited you for daily companionship visits.
asking for an Arabic-speaking companion for his wife.

name: Daniel Okeke
bio: Strong and gentle. Experienced with mobility transfers and supporting elders who need physical assistance. A reassuring, steady presence.

reviews.author: James P.
reviews.text: Strong enough to support my father after his stroke, gentle enough not to bruise his dignity.

reviews.author: Linda M.
reviews.text: Steady, calm, dependable. We never worried with Daniel around.

reviews.author: Catherine O.
reviews.text: Daniel cared for my husband during his final months. I will be grateful forever.

reviews.author: Michael T.
reviews.text: Punctual, respectful, treated our home as if it were his own.

name: Liwayway Cruz
bio: Seasoned live-in volunteer. Known for outstanding dementia care and creating a calm, structured daily routine that brings ease to the whole household.

reviews.author: Amira S.
reviews.text: Liwayway is a gift. My mother's dementia frightens us, but Liwayway brings such calm.

reviews.author: Tariq H.
reviews.text: I have never met someone with more patience. Our lives have changed.

reviews.author: Adel M.
reviews.text: Eighteen years of experience shows in everything she does. We trust her completely.

reviews.author: Hessa B.
reviews.text: A true professional with the heart of a daughter. My mother adores her.

notifications.message: family considering live-in support for dementia care.

name: Noura Mansour
bio: Friendly and patient. Loves cooking traditional meals and keeping elders company through long afternoons.

reviews.author: Khadija A.
reviews.text: Noura makes the most wonderful koshari. My mother looks forward to her visits.

reviews.author: Walid R.
reviews.text: Cheerful and reliable. My father took to her immediately.

reviews.author: Yusuf E.
reviews.text: Noura is gentle and kind. My mother leaves the house feeling lighter.

name: Rajiv Patel
bio: Calm and reliable, with a physiotherapy background. Excellent with post-surgery recovery and helping elders regain confidence with movement.

reviews.author: Vinod K.
reviews.text: Rajiv's physio background was exactly what my father needed after his knee replacement.

reviews.author: Hema D.
reviews.text: Calm and patient. My mother felt at ease with Rajiv from the first visit.

reviews.author: Sanjay P.
reviews.text: He explains exercises clearly and never pushes too hard.

reviews.author: Nikhil S.
reviews.text: Dependable, kind, and skilled. Worth every minute.

name: Grace Mwangi
bio: Cheerful volunteer who genuinely enjoys spending time with elders. A bright, steady companion who brings energy without overwhelm.

reviews.author: Bushra M.
reviews.text: Grace lights up every room she enters. My mother's mood has improved enormously.

reviews.author: Tariq A.
reviews.text: Young but mature beyond her years. Reliable, warm, respectful.

reviews.author: Hadi N.
reviews.text: She brought music back into my mother's days. They sing together in the kitchen.

name: Ahmed Khalil
bio: Patient and respectful male volunteer. Specialises in mobility support and assisting elders with daily activities. A reassuring presence in the home.

reviews.author: Omar Y.
reviews.text: Having a man who spoke Arabic and shared his background meant everything to my father.

reviews.author: Salwa M.
reviews.text: Respectful, dignified, never makes my father feel like a patient.

reviews.author: Karim R.
reviews.text: Fourteen years of experience and you can feel it. Nothing fazes him.

reviews.author: Iman K.
reviews.text: Warm, careful, trustworthy. My grandfather speaks of him as a friend.

notifications.message: asking for medication management twice weekly.

name: Elena Petrova
bio: Trained nurse with strong dementia care experience. A gentle approach and deep patience. Methodical without ever feeling clinical.

reviews.author: Olga V.
reviews.text: Elena's nursing skill is beyond question, but it is her gentleness that makes her exceptional.

reviews.author: Mikhail R.
reviews.text: Methodical, kind, and patient. She remembers details I had forgotten.

reviews.author: Anastasia K.
reviews.text: Speaking Russian with my mother was a quiet gift.

reviews.author: Tatiana M.
reviews.text: My grandmother now waits at the window for Elena to arrive.

notifications.message: backup dementia care during family travel.

name: Joseph Lim
bio: Reliable live-in volunteer with experience in companionship and meal preparation. Treats every elder he supports as part of his own family.

reviews.author: Khalid M.
reviews.text: Joseph has been with my father for two years. He is family.

reviews.author: Reem H.
reviews.text: Steady, devoted, and warm. He learned every detail of my mother's routine.

reviews.author: Sami A.
reviews.text: Joseph treats my father with the kind of respect you usually only see from a son.

reviews.author: Huda K.
reviews.text: He anticipates needs before anyone has to ask.

name: Layla Hassan
bio: Trilingual volunteer with a background in nutrition. Skilled in thoughtful meal preparation and medication reminders.

reviews.author: Mariam J.
reviews.text: Layla redesigned my mother's meals to suit her diabetes without ever making her feel deprived.

reviews.author: Ziad N.
reviews.text: Her French is a delight for my father, who grew up in Beirut and Paris.

reviews.author: Rami K.
reviews.text: Thoughtful, organised, and genuinely warm. My mother adores her.

name: Amelia Reyes
bio: A warm bilingual companion who has spent nearly a decade caring for Emirati elders. Cooks the dishes they grew up with, listens with patience, and walks at their pace.

reviews.author: Khalid F.
reviews.text: Amelia speaks beautiful Arabic and cooks my father's favourite machboos. He looks forward to her visits all week.

reviews.author: Latifa A.
reviews.text: She is gentle, dependable, and never makes my mother feel small.

reviews.author: Hamad B.
reviews.text: Years of experience show. She handles every small change in mood with grace.

reviews.author: Noura K.
reviews.text: We trust Amelia completely with our grandfather.

name: Reema Khoury
bio: Lebanese volunteer with a soft touch and a warmth that puts elders at ease. Brings homemade kibbeh and Friday afternoons full of stories.

reviews.author: Mona K.
reviews.text: Reema feels like a younger cousin who actually shows up. Mother lights up when she hears the door.

reviews.author: Bassel F.
reviews.text: Patient, considerate, and quietly brilliant in the kitchen.

name: Jovita Mendoza
bio: Calm and seasoned, with a particular gift for early-morning routines. Brings rhythm and reassurance to busy households.

reviews.author: Caroline W.
reviews.text: Jovita carries her years of experience lightly. My mother feels safe in her care.

reviews.author: Tom R.
reviews.text: Punctual, organised, and so warm with my father. We trust her completely.

name: Karthik Reddy
bio: Physiotherapy assistant with quiet confidence. Helps elders rebuild their strength after surgery, one careful step at a time.

reviews.author: Anand I.
reviews.text: Karthik's gentle pacing has helped my father walk again. We are deeply grateful.

reviews.author: Sneha K.
reviews.text: Reliable and thoughtful. He explains each exercise in a way my father understands.

name: Mariam Saleh
bio: Gentle Sudanese volunteer who cooks the kind of food that feels like home. Patient companion for elders who need an unhurried day.

reviews.author: Rania A.
reviews.text: Mariam's kindness fills a room. My mother looks forward to their afternoons.

reviews.author: Khalid B.
reviews.text: Wonderful company and a beautiful cook. We are very fond of her.

name: Tanvir Hossain
bio: Tanvir's experience shows in his ease around frailty — careful transfers, steady walks, and conversations that span generations.

reviews.author: Imran H.
reviews.text: Tanvir is steady and patient. My mother has regained her confidence walking again.

reviews.author: Farzana M.
reviews.text: Wonderful with elderly clients. Speaks Bengali with my mother-in-law, which means the world.

name: Yvonne Ndegwa
bio: Bright, joyful Kenyan volunteer who brings light into long afternoons. A wonderful cook and a willing companion for stories.

reviews.author: Sophie W.
reviews.text: Yvonne brought sunshine back to my mother's house. They laugh together every day.

reviews.author: Helen P.
reviews.text: Reliable, cheerful, and so kind. We feel lucky.

name: Omar Sayed
bio: Quiet, dependable Egyptian volunteer who treats every elder as he would his own father. Medication routines down to the minute.

reviews.author: Mona Y.
reviews.text: Omar is meticulous with my father's medications and gentle with his moods. We rest easy.

reviews.author: Hatem R.
reviews.text: Reliable to the hour. Calm to the bone.

name: Anika Rahman
bio: Trained nurse with a calm presence. Specialises in dementia care and the unhurried structure that makes confusion bearable.

reviews.author: Sadia B.
reviews.text: Anika's calm has saved our household. My mother is no longer agitated in the evenings.

reviews.author: Mahbub R.
reviews.text: She brings order without sternness. A gift.

name: Hassan Khoury
bio: Trilingual Lebanese gentleman with a warm laugh and a steady hand. Loved by Arabic-speaking elders who miss the Mediterranean.

reviews.author: Marwan H.
reviews.text: Father speaks French with Hassan all afternoon. It's like having his old life back.

reviews.author: Nadia K.
reviews.text: Wonderful presence. Father walks more confidently with him.

name: Priya Iyer
bio: Bright South Indian volunteer with a nursing background. Brings Tamil-speaking elders the comfort of familiar conversations.

reviews.author: Sundar I.
reviews.text: Priya speaks Tamil with me — and her cooking is like my mother's. A blessing.

reviews.author: Lakshmi K.
reviews.text: Skilled, kind, and trustworthy with medications.

name: Roselle dela Cruz
bio: Cheerful Filipina who turns long days into moments of comfort. Known for Filipino home cooking and a wonderful sense of humour.

reviews.author: Jose dC.
reviews.text: Roselle has become family. Mother eats again and laughs again.

reviews.author: Cecilia M.
reviews.text: Warm, energetic, dependable. The best kind of company.

name: Mohamed Yousef
bio: Tall, patient Sudanese volunteer with a deep voice that elders find reassuring. Strong mobility support and a gentle manner.

reviews.author: Khalid AM.
reviews.text: Mohamed treats my father with such dignity. Father feels safe with him.

reviews.author: Reem AM.
reviews.text: Strong, careful, and never rushed. We trust him completely.

name: Lilian Mwakio
bio: Patient Kenyan companion who delights in cooking with the elders she cares for. Quietly attentive to the details that matter.

reviews.author: Faith G.
reviews.text: Lilian brought her warmth into our home. Mother now insists she stay for lunch every visit.

reviews.author: Daniel M.
reviews.text: Thoughtful and kind. We are very glad to have her.

name: Sajjad Ali
bio: Seasoned Pakistani volunteer with a calm authority. A great match for elders who appreciate respect, routine, and a familiar tongue.

reviews.author: Bilal R.
reviews.text: Father trusts Sajjad like he trusts no one outside the family. Beautiful care.

reviews.author: Ayesha K.
reviews.text: Years of experience show in his calm. We sleep well.

name: Hala Abdelrahman
bio: Dementia care specialist with deep patience and a gentle Arabic voice. Builds calm routines that the whole family can rest in.

reviews.author: Heba M.
reviews.text: Hala turned our crisis into something manageable. Mother is settled again.

reviews.author: Sherif A.
reviews.text: Quietly brilliant with dementia care. We are deeply grateful.

name: Faisal Al-Yousfi
bio: Soft-spoken Yemeni gentleman with a careful manner. Trusted with Emirati elders who value privacy and respectful company.

reviews.author: Abdullah J.
reviews.text: Faisal is courteous in a way that puts father at ease. We're lucky to have him.

reviews.author: Maryam J.
reviews.text: Patient, dignified, and warmly familiar.

name: Indira Perera
bio: Sri Lankan volunteer with the patience of a nurse and the warmth of a daughter. Especially loved by elders far from family.

reviews.author: Helen Y.
reviews.text: Indira is more than a caregiver. She is a friend to my husband.

reviews.author: Robert Y. Jr.
reviews.text: Father waits for her arrival each day. Wonderful care.

name: Sara Awad
bio: Gentle Syrian volunteer who brings the comfort of Levantine cooking and slow afternoons of conversation. Skilled with dementia care.

reviews.author: Rami B.
reviews.text: Sara's Damascus-style cooking has my mother eating properly again. And the company — beyond price.

reviews.author: Nour B.
reviews.text: She is exactly what our family needed.

name: Sunita Tamang
bio: Bright Nepali volunteer with a calm voice and steady hands. Beloved by South Asian elders who feel at home in her care.

reviews.author: Suresh P.
reviews.text: Father speaks Nepali with Sunita as if she were his niece. We can't thank her enough.

reviews.author: Maya R.
reviews.text: Quietly excellent in every way.

name: Dawit Tesfaye
bio: Quietly cheerful Ethiopian volunteer who brings injera, jokes, and patience to the elders he supports. A favourite of children and grandparents alike.

reviews.author: Selam B.
reviews.text: Dawit and father laugh together in Amharic. Watching them is medicine for the whole family.

reviews.author: Yonas T.
reviews.text: Warm, dependable, deeply kind.
